title: Man Ray: When Objects Dream - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: American artist Man Ray (1890–1976) was a visionary known for his radical experiments that pushed the limits of photography, painting, sculpture, and film.

title: Superfine: Tailoring Black Style - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Superfine: Tailoring Black Style presents a cultural and historical examination of Black style over three hundred years through the concept of dandyism.

title: The Roof Garden Commission: Jennie C. Jones, Ensemble - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: For the 2025 Roof Garden Commission, Jennie C. Jones presents Ensemble, acoustic sculptures exploring sound, form, and the continuity of art history.

title: Recasting the Past: The Art of Chinese Bronzes, 1100–1900 - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit reexamines 12th–19th century Chinese bronzes, once seen as imitations, revealing their unique artistry through 200 works from global collections.

title: Allegory and Abstraction: Selections from the Department of Drawings and Prints - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The exhibition highlights how artists like Matisse, Bourgeois, and others used allegory and abstraction to convey emotion, narrative, and layered meaning.

title: The Genesis Facade Commission: Jeffrey Gibson, The Animal That Therefore I Am - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Acclaimed artist Jeffrey Gibson (born 1972) invites reflection on the interconnected relationships between all living beings and the environment.

title: Witnessing Humanity: The Art of John Wilson - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The exhibition features over 100 artworks made over the course of Wilson’s career, many of which have not been shown before.

title: The Magic of Marbling: The Art of Karli Frigge - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Karli Frigge (b. 1943), a leading paper marbler, created innovative works (1960–2000). This exhibit showcases her sample books, guides, and publications.

title: P. S. Art 2025: Celebrating the Creative Spirit of New York City Kids - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: P. S. Art is an annual celebration of the artistic achievement of remarkable New York City Public School students and their dedicated art teachers.

title: Baseball Cards from the Collection of Jefferson R. Burdick - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The Jefferson R. Burdick collection at The Met features 100+ historic baseball cards from the 19th century to the 1950s, showcasing legends and varied styles.
